scenarios.brief: You are MV Blue Duck (MMSI: *********). Engine room fire is out of control. 6 persons on board, position 32°05'N 034°45'E. Ship is in immediate danger.
You are SY Sea Otter (MMSI: *********). Hit a submerged object, hull breached, taking water fast. 4 persons on board, position 31°50'N 034°30'E. Vessel will sink within minutes.
You are MV Northern Star (MMSI: *********). Collided with another vessel, severe damage to bow, taking water. 12 persons on board, position 33°10'N 035°02'E.

facts.nature: Collided with another vessel and taking water

scenarios.brief: You are MV River Hawk (MMSI: *********). Engine-room fire has spread to the bridge and is uncontrollable. 7 persons on board, position 32°25'N 034°48'E. Make the MAYDAY call — you must abandon ship to the liferaft immediately after.

facts.nature: Fire out of control, abandoning ship

scenarios.brief: You are MV Aurora (MMSI: *********). Catastrophic hull breach after grounding, vessel listing heavily and capsizing. 9 persons on board, position 31°40'N 034°25'E. Make the MAYDAY call — you must abandon ship to the liferaft immediately after.

You are SY Wandering Albatross (MMSI: *********). Mast came down in heavy weather and is lying over the side with rigging in the water. Vessel disabled and drifting. 4 persons on board, position 32°35'N 034°15'E.
You are SY Red Fox (MMSI: *********). Engine has failed, drifting toward a traffic separation scheme. Not in immediate danger, but need a tow. 3 persons on board, position 32°15'N 034°40'E.

You are MV Grey Whale (MMSI: *********). Crew member with severe chest pain — suspected cardiac event. Address RCC Haifa. 8 persons on board, position 31°45'N 034°20'E. Patient: male, 52, temp 37.2°C, BP 160/100, conscious but very weak. Crew has given aspirin and rigged oxygen. Make the MEDICO call (DSC + Ch 16), then continue on the working channel.

facts.patientStatus: Severe chest pain, conscious but very weak, suspected cardiac event
facts.actionsTaken: Aspirin administered, oxygen rigged, patient resting

scenarios.brief: You are MV Cargo Ranger (MMSI: *********). A crew member fell from the upper deck and is unconscious. No nearby coast station designated — broadcast to All Stations. 14 persons on board, position 32°50'N 035°10'E. Patient: male, 28, temp 36.0°C, BP 90/60, unresponsive with suspected head injury. Crew immobilised the head/neck and is monitoring breathing. Make the MEDICO call (DSC + Ch 16), then continue on the working channel.

facts.patientStatus: Unconscious after fall from upper deck, suspected head injury
facts.actionsTaken: Head and neck immobilised, breathing monitored, no medication given

scenarios.brief: You are SY Blue Marlin (MMSI: *********). A crew member has uncontrolled bleeding from a deep galley laceration. Address RCC Haifa. 5 persons on board, position 32°10'N 034°35'E. Patient: female, 41, temp 36.8°C, BP 100/65, conscious and pale. Direct pressure applied, tourniquet rigged on upper arm. Make the MEDICO call (DSC + Ch 16), then continue on the working channel.

facts.patientStatus: Uncontrolled bleeding from deep galley laceration, conscious and pale
facts.actionsTaken: Direct pressure applied, tourniquet rigged on upper arm

scenarios.brief: You are MV Silver Arrow (MMSI: *********). Lost steering, vessel manoeuvring with difficulty in busy shipping lane. Position 32°30'N 034°55'E.

facts.assistance: Request other vessels to keep clear

scenarios.brief: You are MV Cape Runner. Sighted a partly submerged shipping container drifting in a busy shipping lane, hazard to navigation. Position 32°20'N 034°50'E.
You are MV Osprey. Lateral buoy reported unlit at the harbour approach. Position 31°55'N 034°35'E.
You are MV Storm Petrel. Observed sudden gale-force squalls in the area, hazard to small craft. Position 33°00'N 035°00'E.
You abandoned ship and are aboard the Albatross life raft. Your Radar SART has just activated and is being tracked by passing ships. 4 persons on board. Make the distress call.

facts.sartAddressee: Ship who received my Radar SART

scenarios.brief: You are aboard the Petrel life raft and sight a red-hulled cargo ship on the horizon, off the ship's starboard side. 5 persons on board. Make the distress call to the sighted ship.

facts.position: I am on your starboard side

scenarios.brief: RCC Haifa acknowledged your earlier MAYDAY. Respond to RCC and request fast vessels to evacuate your crew. You are sailboat Sea Otter.

facts.actionRequest: Send fast ships to the distress area to evacuate the crew
